title: Proprium Sanctorum: Zusatz-Homilien des Ms. Vernon fol. CCXV ff. zur nördlichen Sammlung der Dominicalia evangelia
summary: The repository XML represents University of Michigan CME item CME00064, an encoding of Carl Horstmann's 1888 edition of a late-fourteenth-century supplementary sanctorale homily sequence in the Vernon Manuscript. Horstmann prints twenty-eight complete Middle English verse homilies in two journal runs, pp. 83-114 and 299-321. The manuscript sequence at Vernon fols. 215v-227v has thirty-five feast positions: twenty-nine full homilies and six title-only cross-references. Horstmann retains the six references but omits the full Invention of the Cross homily beginning Erat homo; the encoded selection therefore must not be mistaken for the entire manuscript sequence. The complete 710-canvas Vernon object, not a cropped work extract, is cached and the selected span is mapped within it. The complete 4,616,920,131-byte Oxford facsimile/transcription dataset is also cached and integrity-checked, with exact internal transcription and image paths recorded for fols. 215v-227v. The parallel sequence in British Library Add MS 22283 is collateral evidence, not a documented source for the 1888 transcription; current catalog structure places it at fols. 19v(a)-30v(a) and appears to omit the Exaltation of the Cross homily present in Vernon.
review_notes: The medieval sequence, each manuscript witness, Horstmann's editions, U-M encoding, repository artifact, and modern facsimiles are modeled as separate layers.

Complete local facsimile bundles retain the provider's entire physical or bibliographic object. work_portion locators point to the corpus selection without redefining a full manuscript or journal as a cropped extract.
The article's traditional page citation is corrected to pp. 83-114 and 299-321. Printed pp. 322-326 are unrelated reviews.

Horstmann's 1881 claim that Add MS 22283 is a mere copy of Vernon is retained as a historical, tentative claim. Current catalog data assign the target sequence to Add Scribe 1; the scribe shared with Vernon is Add Scribe 2 elsewhere in the codex.

The XML's 2016 revision note reports removal of fragments from the middle and end, but does not identify them. No claim equates those unspecified fragments with the demonstrable Horstmann omission of Erat homo.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00064
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00064; the file is 261,795 bytes.
The XML contains one DIV1 and twenty-eight DIV2 divisions, 5,582 L elements in sixty-four LG elements, fifty-five PB milestones, and languages enm, ger, and lat.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00064
entities.description: A manually keyed and proofread TEI-like representation of Horstmann's complete twenty-eight-homily selection, including Latin Gospel rubrics, German editorial framing and notes, and printed page milestones.
notes: The 2016 revision history records structural normalization and two unidentified fragment removals; the currently encoded text still contains all twenty-eight homilies printed by Horstmann.

entities.label: U-M CME00064 source-page TIFF selection
entities.description: A fifty-five-file one-bit TIFF bundle corresponding to Horstmann's two article runs. It is a selected reproduction of printed source pages, not a manuscript facsimile and not a complete journal volume.
entities.reproduction_format: 55 one-bit TIFF page images in a ZIP archive

entities.label: Carl Horstmann's 1888 Proprium Sanctorum selection

bibliographic.citation: Carl Horstmann, 'Proprium Sanctorum: Zusatz-Homilien des Ms. Vernon fol. CCXV ff. zur nördlichen Sammlung der Dominicalia evangelia,' Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen 81 (42. Jahrgang, 1888): 83-114, 299-321.
bibliographic.title: Proprium Sanctorum: Zusatz-Homilien des Ms. Vernon fol. CCXV ff. zur nördlichen Sammlung der Dominicalia evangelia
bibliographic.container_title: Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen

notes: This is the immediate printed source represented by CME00064.

The first run ends on p. 114 with the continuation notice Schluss folgt; the article resumes on p. 299 and its target text ends on p. 321. The following cue Hou a man schal lyue parfytly introduces another manuscript work and is not part of this selection.

Horstmann prints twenty-eight full homilies, retains six incipit cross-references in their manuscript positions, and editorially omits the full repeated Erat homo homily for the Invention of the Cross.

entities.label: BSB complete IIIF sequence for Archiv volume 81 (1888)
entities.description: A 502-canvas provider-published color sequence preserving the complete exposed journal volume, not only Horstmann's article.
entities.reproduction_format: 502-canvas IIIF Presentation 2 object; cached as a ZIP containing all width-1600 JPEG derivatives, provider manifest, and source inventory

entities.label: Internet Archive complete PDF for Archiv volume 81 (1888)
entities.description: A complete 499-page Google-derived scan of the journal volume, with cached metadata, scan structure, and complete OCR sidecars.
entities.reproduction_format: 499-page PDF plus Internet Archive metadata, scandata, and DjVu XML OCR

entities.label: Carl Horstmann's Altenglische Legenden, Neue Folge (1881)

bibliographic.citation: Carl Horstmann, Altenglische Legenden, Neue Folge (Heilbronn: Gebr. Henninger, 1881), pp. LXXIV-LXXVII.
bibliographic.title: Altenglische Legenden, Neue Folge

notes: The cited introductory pages identify the feast sequence, describe the prologue and southern-addition hypotheses, and compare the Vernon and Simeon codices. They are a scholarly guide cited by the 1888 article, not its immediate printed text.

entities.label: Internet Archive/Google complete PDF of Horstmann's 1881 volume
entities.description: A complete 686-page PDF with Internet Archive metadata and scan-structure sidecars.
entities.reproduction_format: 686-page PDF plus item metadata and scandata sidecars

entities.label: Carl Horstmann's Die Evangelien-Geschichten der Homiliensammlung des Ms. Vernon (1877)

bibliographic.citation: Carl Horstmann, 'Die Evangelien-Geschichten der Homiliensammlung des Ms. Vernon,' Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen 57 (31. Jahrgang, 1877): 241-316.
bibliographic.title: Die Evangelien-Geschichten der Homiliensammlung des Ms. Vernon
bibliographic.container_title: Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen

notes: The 1888 Mary Magdalene homily notes that its lines 23-100 parallel the first Gospel story printed here at p. 241. This is a cited parallel, not an upstream source for the whole 1888 edition.

entities.label: BSB complete IIIF sequence for Archiv volume 57 (1877)
entities.description: A 500-canvas complete journal sequence containing Horstmann's related 1877 article.
entities.reproduction_format: 500-canvas IIIF Presentation 2 object cached as a complete width-1600 JPEG bundle

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS. Eng. poet. a. 1 (Vernon Manuscript)
entities.description: A monumental late-fourteenth-century parchment codex. The complete supplementary sanctorale sequence is at current fols. 215v-227v; the twenty-eight homilies printed by Horstmann occur there around one full omitted homily and six internal cross-references.

holding.notes: The current record tentatively gives England, West Midlands; origin and patronage remain debated.

physical_description.extent: viii + 342 + i surviving leaves, incomplete; originally viii + 413, with 71 leaves thought lost
physical_description.format: Large codex, normally quires of eight; nineteenth-century crushed russet leather over bevelled oak boards
notes: The target sequence occupies a small portion of a codex containing more than 350 texts; it begins on a leaf also ending Piers Toller and ends on a leaf also beginning How a Man Shall Live Perfectly.

entities.label: Complete Digital Bodleian Vernon Manuscript object
entities.description: The complete 710-canvas object exposed by Digital Bodleian, including covers, spine, flyleaves, blanks, every surviving digitized leaf, and adjacent texts. No dedicated edge views are exposed.
entities.reproduction_format: 710 color IIIF canvases; complete provider object cached at an effective width of 1600 pixels

entities.label: Scase and Kennedy complete Vernon facsimile/transcription dataset
entities.description: The complete Bodleian Digital Texts 3 facsimile edition deposited in ORA: a 4.3 GB ZIP containing high-resolution images of the entire manuscript, a live transcription, page descriptions, and specialist essays.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete downloadable 4.3 GB ZIP facsimile edition with high-resolution images, complete transcription, descriptions, and essays

entities.label: Wendy Scase's complete Vernon Manuscript transcription
entities.description: The complete scholarly transcription distributed within the ORA Vernon facsimile dataset and exposed as page annotations in Digital Bodleian.

entities.label: London, British Library, Add MS 22283 (Simeon Manuscript)
entities.description: A very large late-fourteenth-century English parchment miscellany preserving a parallel supplementary sanctorale sequence at fols. 19v(a)-30v(a), copied by Add Scribe 1. Current catalog structure moves from the Nativity of the Virgin homily to Matthew and appears not to contain the Vernon Exaltation of the Cross homily in this sequence.

holding.notes: Origin is given as England, perhaps north Worcestershire.

physical_description.extent: 172 folios plus unfoliated modern paper leaves and five inserted modern paper leaves; many leaves lost or remounted
physical_description.format: Very large miscellany copied by three scribes
notes: Target execution is Add Scribe 1, fols. 1r-61v. Add Scribe 2, not the target scribe, is identified by the current catalog as Vernon Scribe 2.

entities.label: Former British Library Digitised Manuscripts object for Add MS 22283
entities.description: A former full-display endpoint for manuscript images. The current British Library catalog states that images are unavailable, and no current public whole-object image delivery was found.

object.description: description and feast identification for the Proprium sequence

subject.description: comparison of Vernon and the Simeon manuscript

scope.notes: Horstmann calls Add MS 22283 a mere copy of Vernon and says the Proprium is complete and verbatim. Current catalog evidence weakens that formulation: the target was copied by Add Scribe 1, not the scribe shared with Vernon, and current item structure appears to omit Vernon's Exaltation of the Cross homily.

assertion.notes: Retained as a historical scholarly claim requiring renewed textual and codicological review.

scope.notes: Horstmann says the earlier version preserves northern traces less fully erased. This is a scoped parallel, not a source relation for the other homilies.

ZIP integrity passed with no errors. The archive contains 7,519 entries, including 7,375 non-directory files and 4,669,625,567 uncompressed bytes.

The dataset includes Vernon transcription.xml, Vernon description.xml, four specialist essay PDFs, a complete web application, multiple image resolutions for the manuscript, page-level transcriptions, and page descriptions.

notes: The complete dataset is cached without extraction as a substitute work-only object; work locators point into the full archive.

access.format: Former web page images; current delivery unavailable

access.access_method: British Library digitisation service online order form; request a quotation for expert color photography of the entire surviving object
access.contact: Use the online order form; written-permission enquiries: permissions@bl.uk; the digitisation page also directs reproduction licensing questions to licensing@bl.uk
access.format: Ask for color TIFFs or highest-resolution archival image files; rare material may require expert photography rather than scanning
notes: Request the whole physical object: covers/boards, spine, pastedowns, all beginning and end leaves, every surviving manuscript leaf recto and verso, five inserted modern paper leaves, flyleaves and blanks, and edges when the service offers them. Identify the work separately at fols. 19v(a)-30v(a).

A rough standard-rate baseline for 412 leaf-side surfaces is about GBP 3,007.45 (first TIFF GBP 27.70 plus 411 subsequent images at GBP 7.25), before binding furniture, expert photography, handling, administration, or complex-request fees. Only a quote can establish the actual whole-object price.
British Library terms state that supplied images may not be copied or reproduced without written permission; request separate permission for any repository publication or commercial use.

rights.component: Medieval text and underlying homily sequence
rights.basis: The manuscript text and anonymous late-medieval works predate copyright by centuries; this does not determine rights in modern photography, transcription, or editorial matter.

rights.jurisdiction: United States and jurisdictions using ordinary author-term/publication-term copyright rules
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1888 and its editor died in 1912; the historical edition is public domain by age. Modern scan rights and terms remain separate.

rights.jurisdiction: United States and jurisdictions using ordinary author-term/publication-term copyright rules
rights.basis: Published in 1881; public domain by age. Reproduction-provider terms remain separate.

rights.basis: Published in 1877; public domain by age. Reproduction-provider terms remain separate.
The exact U-M item applies the No Copyright-United States rights statement to the digitized historical material.

rights.component: Kyvero-Press/CME repository artifact and software-layer contributions
rights.basis: The pinned CME repository contains an Unlicense dedication for software/source-code form. This repository-level statement is not treated as relicensing third-party photographs or separately protected scholarly content.

rights.component: U-M selected TIFF reproductions of public-domain article pages
rights.basis: U-M applies No Copyright-United States to the CME historical materials; the TIFF bundle reproduces an 1888 public-domain edition.

rights.component: BSB page images, IIIF derivatives, metadata, and local complete bundle
rights.basis: BSB labels the digital object No Copyright - Non-Commercial Use Only. The statement does not grant commercial reuse; the noncommercial condition is preserved as a provider term.
notes: Do not publish these images in a commercially reusable repository or print-on-demand product without resolving the provider restriction.

rights.component: BSB page images, IIIF derivatives, metadata, and local complete bundle
rights.basis: BSB labels the digital object No Copyright - Non-Commercial Use Only; the noncommercial condition remains a provider term.

rights.component: Internet Archive-hosted Google scan, OCR, and metadata
rights.basis: Internet Archive item metadata applies the Public Domain Mark to the scan, while embedded Google frontmatter requests personal noncommercial use, attribution, and no automated querying. Both layers are recorded rather than treating access as unrestricted commercial permission.
notes: Private research caching is distinct from repository redistribution. Prefer the public-domain text layer or obtain a scan with clear commercial terms for print-on-demand use.

rights.component: Internet Archive-hosted Google scan and metadata
rights.basis: Internet Archive metadata identifies the work as not in copyright in the United States, while embedded Google frontmatter requests personal noncommercial use and attribution.

rights.component: Digital Bodleian manuscript photographs and cached IIIF derivatives
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and CC BY-NC 4.0 license terms
rights.basis: The exact IIIF manifest attributes the photographs to Bodleian Libraries and applies CC BY-NC 4.0.
notes: The private gitignored cache does not authorize repository publication or commercial print-on-demand use of the images; commercial use requires permission beyond CC BY-NC.

rights.component: Complete facsimile/transcription dataset, application, essays, descriptions, and high-resolution images
rights.basis: The ORA record names Bodleian Library Publishing as copyright holder, 2019. In the absence of a more permissive item license, ORA permits one downloaded or printed copy for private study or research for noncommercial purposes and prohibits profit-making use.
notes: Keep the cache private and gitignored. Link the ORA record rather than redistributing the ZIP; obtain permission before repository publication or commercial use.

rights.component: Modern complete transcription and scholarly apparatus
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and ORA/Digital Bodleian terms
rights.basis: The modern transcription and scholarly apparatus are part of the copyrighted Vernon digital edition; public access is not an unrestricted commercial reuse license.

rights.component: Medieval manuscript text and underlying historical works
rights.basis: The manuscript text predates copyright by centuries. This does not determine rights in modern photographs, catalog text, or ordered reproductions.

rights.component: Former British Library web manuscript images
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and unknown legacy provider terms
rights.basis: The former image endpoint is unavailable and its exact item-level reuse terms could not be recovered.

rights.component: New images supplied through the British Library digitisation service
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and British Library supply/permission terms
rights.basis: British Library imaging terms state that requested images may not be copied or reproduced without written permission; additional permission and fees may apply.
notes: Ask permissions@bl.uk expressly about private research use, publication of images in the git repository, and commercial or noncommercial print-on-demand use.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A manuscript sequence with twenty-nine complete homilies and six title-only cross-references
editorial_practices.representation: Twenty-eight complete homilies plus the six cross-references
editorial_practices.description: Horstmann omits the complete repeated Erat homo homily at the Invention of the Cross while retaining its incipit/reference position. He also supplies feast identifications because the manuscript itself does not label the feasts.
editorial_practices.scope: Entire edited selection; omitted source passage at Vernon fols. 221r-222r

editorial_practices.source_feature: Scribal abbreviations in the Vernon text
editorial_practices.representation: Italic letters within Middle English words appear to represent editorially supplied expansion characters
editorial_practices.description: Comparison of the print transcription, U-M italic HI spans, and manuscript images supports abbreviation expansion as the principal function of embedded italics, but Horstmann's explicit convention statement has not yet been located.
editorial_practices.scope: Italic characters embedded throughout the Middle English transcription
notes: Use the manuscript image or Scase transcription to verify individual expansions; do not treat italics generically as emphasis.

editorial_practices.representation: Footnote relating Mary Magdalene lines 23-100 to the 1877 text
editorial_practices.description: Horstmann identifies the scoped parallel and notes different preservation of northern linguistic traces; this does not document a full collation against Add MS 22283.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Printed article structure, headings, trailing cue, and unidentified fragments in an earlier encoding state
editorial_practices.representation: Twenty-eight DIV2 homily units; title references represented as EPIGRAPH Q elements; following work excluded
editorial_practices.description: The 2016 revision converted several former divisions to epigraphs and reports removing a middle fragment and an end fragment. The exact deleted content is not specified, so those changes are not equated with Horstmann's separately demonstrable Erat homo omission.

evidence.summary: Announces public availability of the complete Scase transcription alongside all Vernon images in Digital Bodleian and points to the ORA XML and project data.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Add MS 22283, record 032-002034756.
evidence.summary: Current authority for shelfmark, ARK, date 1360-1399, tentative north-Worcestershire origin, extent, inserted leaves, physical condition, three scribes, target sequence at fols. 19v(a)-30v(a), its Add Scribe 1 execution, and unavailable image status.

evidence.citation: British Library Digitised Manuscripts legacy FullDisplay endpoint for Add MS 22283.
evidence.summary: Documents the former public image endpoint; current access fails and the replacement catalog reports images unavailable.

evidence.citation: British Library, Reader registration and manuscript consultation guidance.
evidence.summary: Explains Reader Pass registration and additional introduction requirements for consulting manuscripts onsite.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation services.
evidence.summary: Current route for requesting and quoting reproduction of collection material; rare manuscripts may require expert photography rather than ordinary scanning.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services price list 2025/26.
evidence.summary: Lists TIFF pricing of GBP 27.70 for the first image and GBP 7.25 for each subsequent image, plus other service and complex-order charges; actual whole-manuscript cost requires a quote.

evidence.citation: British Library, Imaging Services terms and conditions, updated January 2026.
evidence.summary: States that supplied images may not be copied or reproduced without written permission and that fees may apply; permissions enquiries are directed to permissions@bl.uk. The service page separately directs reproduction licensing questions to licensing@bl.uk.

evidence.citation: Anne B. Thompson, ed., The Northern Homily Cycle, Middle English Texts Series (2008), online introduction.
evidence.summary: Current scholarly synthesis for homily form and the textual tradition: Vernon and Simeon preserve the first major late-fourteenth-century, Midland expansion of the Northern Homily Cycle, adding feast-day material.
notes: The modern edition is copyrighted and linked for research; it is not cached or redistributed.
Claims about the original early-fourteenth-century northern cycle are not automatically transferred to the supplementary sanctorale additions.

open_questions.question: What exact middle and end fragments did the 2016 U-M revision remove?
next_steps: Compare pre- and post-2016 encoding snapshots or proofsheets if recoverable
Do not equate the unnamed removals with Erat homo without documentary evidence

open_questions.question: What is the exact textual and codicological relationship between the Vernon and Add MS 22283 supplementary sanctorale sequences?
next_steps: Obtain or consult complete Add MS 22283 images
Reassess Horstmann's mere-copy claim using current scribal assignments

open_questions.question: Does Add MS 22283 truly lack the Exaltation of the Cross homily Nunc iudicium in this sequence, or is the omission only in the current catalog structure?
next_steps: Ask the British Library to confirm the item boundary
Compare any Scase/Vernon project notes on the Simeon witness

open_questions.question: Will the British Library restore public Add MS 22283 images, and what exact reuse permission would govern a new whole-manuscript reproduction?
next_steps: Submit a whole-object digitisation quote request
Request written repository-publication and commercial/noncommercial print-on-demand permissions separately
Ask whether the former digital surrogate can be restored

open_questions.question: Can modern scholarship refine or confirm Horstmann's distinct northwestern-prologue and southern-homilist authorship/localization hypotheses?
next_steps: Review current scholarship on the Vernon/Simeon first expansion and the Proprium sequence
Keep dialect, composition place, and manuscript production place distinct

open_questions.question: Did Horstmann explicitly define every italic insertion in the transcription as an abbreviation expansion?
next_steps: Search Horstmann's editorial prefaces for a convention statement
Use manuscript images and the complete Scase transcription to verify individual forms
